message:
feat(core): add wavelet_tree.rs — rank/select/quantile queries on byte sequences (ft-283h4.36)

Wavelet tree with O(log sigma) rank, select, range count, and range
quantile queries. Uses bitvector with precomputed rank index. Ideal for
analyzing terminal output byte distributions and scrollback character
frequency queries. 16 inline tests + 13 property-based tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"ft-283h4.25","title":"Implement interval_tree.rs — augmented interval tree for efficient range queries","description":"# Interval Tree for Range Queries

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement an augmented interval tree (based on red-black or balanced BST) for O(log n) interval operations.

## Data Structure
- Augmented balanced BST where each node stores an interval [low, high) and a max-endpoint in subtree
- O(log n) insert/delete
- O(log n + k) overlap queries (k = number of overlapping intervals)
- O(log n) stabbing queries (point-in-interval)

## Use Cases in FrankenTerm
- Track active scrollback regions for viewport management
- Find overlapping time ranges for session replay correlation
- Range-based pane activity windows for scheduling
- Memory region tracking for buffer management

## Implementation
- Generic over interval endpoints: Ord + Clone
- Iterative (not recursive) to avoid stack overflow on large trees
- Serde serializable for persistence
- Comprehensive inline tests (25+)
- Proptest property-based tests (30+) verifying:
  - All overlap queries return correct results
  - Stabbing queries consistent with overlap queries
  - Insert/delete maintain BST and augmentation invariants
  - Merge of interval sets is associative/commutative
  - Serde roundtrip preserves structure

## Files

 {"id":"ft-283h4.26","title":"Implement fenwick_tree.rs — Binary Indexed Tree for O(log n) prefix sum queries","description":"# Fenwick Tree (Binary Indexed Tree)

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement a Binary Indexed Tree (Fenwick Tree) for efficient prefix sum queries and point updates in O(log n) time with O(n) space.

## Use Cases
- Cumulative output byte tracking across pane timelines
- Range frequency queries for monitoring dashboards
- Rank queries for quantile estimation
- Histogram bucket aggregation in telemetry pipelines

## API Surface
- FenwickTree::new(n) — create with n elements
- update(index, delta) — add delta to position
- prefix_sum(index) — sum of elements [0..=index]
- range_sum(left, right) — sum of elements [left..=right]
- find_kth(k) — find smallest index where prefix_sum >= k
- stats() / config / serde roundtrip

## Testing
- 25+ inline unit tests
- 30+ proptest properties: prefix sum correctness vs naive, range sum decomposition, update commutativity, find_kth monotonicity, serde roundtrip","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:21:15.378944Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T02:26:02.212569Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T02:26:02.212519Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.26","depends_on_id":"ft-283h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:21:15.378944Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-283h4.27","title":"Implement adaptive_radix_tree.rs — ART for space-efficient prefix search","description":"# Adaptive Radix Tree (ART) for Prefix Matching

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement an Adaptive Radix Tree for space-efficient sorted key-value storage with O(k) operations (k = key length).

## Data Structure
- Radix trie with 4 node sizes that adapt to branching factor:
  - Node4: up to 4 children (sorted array)
  - Node16: up to 16 children (sorted array with SIMD-friendly layout)
  - Node48: up to 48 children (index array + child array)
  - Node256: full 256-entry array for dense branches
- Path compression (lazy expansion) for long shared prefixes
- O(k) search/insert/delete where k = key length

## Use Cases in FrankenTerm
- Fast prefix search on agent command/event history
- Efficient trie-based pattern deduplication for event streams
- Memory-efficient sorted index for terminal session data
- Auto-complete for command history

 {"id":"ft-283h4.28","title":"Implement segment_tree.rs — lazy segment tree for range queries and range updates","description":"# Segment Tree with Lazy Propagation

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement a segment tree supporting O(log n) range queries and range updates with lazy propagation.

## Use Cases
- Range min/max/sum queries on telemetry time series
- Range update operations for batch metric adjustments
- Interval scheduling and occupancy tracking
- Complement to Fenwick Tree (which only supports point updates)

## API Surface
- SegmentTree::new(values) — build from slice in O(n)
- query(left, right) — range aggregate in O(log n)
- update(index, value) — point update in O(log n)
- range_update(left, right, delta) — range update in O(log n)
- Configurable aggregate operations (sum, min, max)
- Stats / config / serde roundtrip

## Testing

- 30+ proptest properties","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:26:32.123367Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T02:29:36.921458Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T02:29:36.921405Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.28","depends_on_id":"ft-283h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:26:32.123367Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-283h4.29","title":"Implement trie.rs — compact prefix trie for command completion and path matching","description":"# Trie (Prefix Tree)

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement a compact trie for efficient prefix operations on strings.

## Use Cases
- Command completion and auto-suggest in terminal UI
- Path prefix matching for pane routing
- Shared-prefix deduplication in session names
- Fast longest-common-prefix queries

## API Surface
- Trie::new() — empty trie
- insert(key) — add a key
- contains(key) — exact match
- starts_with(prefix) — prefix exists
- keys_with_prefix(prefix) — iterate matching keys
- remove(key) — delete key
- len() / is_empty()
- longest_common_prefix(key) — LCP query
- stats() / config / serde roundtrip

 {"id":"ft-283h4.3","title":"Formal mux protocol verification via TLA+","description":"# Formal Mux Protocol Verification via TLA+

## Why This Is Novel
No terminal emulator in existence has formal protocol verification. This is the purest expression of alien-artifact-coding. TLA+ finds bugs that no amount of testing can find — Lamport proved this mathematically. Amazon uses TLA+ for S3, DynamoDB, EBS, and found critical bugs in all of them.

## What We Model

### Spec 1: PDU Exchange Protocol
States: Idle, Sending, Receiving, Error, Reconnecting
Properties to verify:
- **Safety**: Messages are never lost (every sent PDU is eventually received or error-reported)
- **Ordering**: PDUs are delivered in sequence order
- **No duplicates**: Each PDU is processed exactly once
- **Liveness**: Every operation eventually completes (no indefinite blocking)

### Spec 2: Snapshot/Restore Lifecycle
States: Normal, Capturing, Writing, Stored, Restoring, Restored
Properties to verify:
- **Consistency**: Snapshot captures a consistent point-in-time state
- **Atomicity**: Restore is all-or-nothing (no partial state)
- **No data loss**: snapshot(state) followed by restore() reproduces state exactly
- **Idempotency**: restore(restore(snapshot)) == restore(snapshot)

### Spec 3: Concurrent Pane Management
Multiple panes being created, destroyed, resized, focused simultaneously.
Properties to verify:
- **No orphans**: Every created pane is eventually destroyed or persisted
- **No leaks**: Resources (FDs, memory) are freed when panes close
- **No deadlocks**: No circular wait conditions under any interleaving
- **Liveness**: Every create/destroy operation completes in bounded steps

### Spec 4: WAL Correctness (if #2 is implemented)
Properties:
- **Durability**: Mutations written to WAL survive crashes
- **Replay equivalence**: Replaying WAL produces identical state to original
- **Compaction safety**: Compaction preserves all information

## Deliverables
- `docs/formal/mux_protocol.tla` — PDU exchange spec
- `docs/formal/snapshot_lifecycle.tla` — Snapshot spec
- `docs/formal/concurrent_panes.tla` — Concurrency spec
- `docs/formal/wal_correctness.tla` — WAL spec (if applicable)
- `docs/formal/VERIFICATION_RESULTS.md` — Properties verified, model checking results, any bugs found
- The TLA+ specs serve as the AUTHORITATIVE protocol documentation

## State Space
The mux protocol has ~15-20 states, 5-8 actions per state. Model checking with TLC is tractable (millions of states in minutes). We're not verifying a distributed system — just a client-server protocol with finite state.

## Tooling
- TLA+ Toolbox or `tlc` CLI for model checking
- PlusCal (algorithmic language) for easier spec writing
- Apalache (symbolic model checker) for infinite-state properties

## Testing
- Model checking all specs with TLC — must pass with zero violations
- Any bugs found are documented and fixed in the Rust implementation
- Specs updated when protocol changes

## Cost/Benefit
- Cost: ~2-3 days for a developer familiar with TLA+
- Benefit: Mathematical certainty about protocol correctness, forever
- ROI: One prevented data-loss bug pays for the entire effort

## Depends on
- wa-2umk2 (FrankenTerm in-tree) — need to understand the protocol to model it
- Does NOT depend on implementation work — verification is orthogonal

---

## Cross-References
- **wa-2dd4s.5** (FrankenMux wire protocol): The TLA+ specs in this bead formally model the wire protocol defined in wa-2dd4s.5. Any protocol changes in wa-2dd4s.5 must be reflected here, and any bugs found by TLC model checking should feed back as protocol fixes.
- **wa-283h4.13** (Self-stabilizing mux protocol): The self-stabilization convergence invariants should be modeled as TLA+ temporal properties here. Spec 1 (PDU exchange) should include self-stabilization reconciliation messages as additional actions.

## TLC Model Checker Configuration
Run TLC with explicit state space bounds to keep model checking tractable:
- Max states: 10M (sufficient for protocol with ~20 states × 8 actions)
- Max depth: 50 transitions (bounds liveness checking)
- Symmetry reduction: apply symmetry sets for pane IDs to reduce state space by N!
- Liveness checking: use `-lncheck` flag for temporal property verification
- CI integration: `tlc -workers auto -deadlock mux_protocol.tla` in GitHub Actions

## Runtime Property Validation via Proptest
While TLA+/TLC verifies the model, proptest validates that the Rust implementation matches the model:

```
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn pdu_ordering_preserved(pdus in prop::collection::vec(arb_pdu(), 1..100)) {
        // Send PDUs through codec pipeline, verify arrival order matches send order
    }

    #[test]
    fn snapshot_restore_roundtrip(state in arb_mux_state()) {
        // snapshot(state) -> restore() -> assert_eq!(original, restored)
    }

    #[test]
    fn no_duplicate_delivery(pdus in prop::collection::vec(arb_pdu(), 1..50),
                             drop_indices in prop::collection::vec(0..50usize, 0..10)) {
        // Simulate drops and retransmits, verify each PDU delivered exactly once
    }

    #[test]
    fn convergence_from_arbitrary_state(
        initial in arb_corrupted_mux_state(),
        reconciliation_rounds in 1..5u32
    ) {
        // Verify protocol converges to legitimate state within bounded rounds
    }
}
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletBarn","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:06:41.536788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:40:33.381750Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-283h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2umk2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:29:20Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":190,"issue_id":"ft-283h4.3","author":"ScarletBarn","text":"Implemented formal verification artifacts under docs/formal: mux_protocol.tla (updated with bounded MaxMsgId + fairness Spec), snapshot_lifecycle.tla, concurrent_panes.tla, wal_correctness.tla, with companion TLC configs and docs/formal/VERIFICATION_RESULTS.md. TLC results: mux_protocol PASS (1,395/1,125 states), snapshot_lifecycle PASS (102/64), concurrent_panes safety PASS (22,980,985/1,748,872), wal_correctness PASS (92,841/36,408). Caveat documented: concurrent_panes liveness checks were deferred due existential fairness starvation artifacts with set-queue scheduling; liveness formulas remain in module for follow-on queue-model refinement.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:40:33Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-283h4.30","title":"Implement rope.rs — balanced text rope for efficient scrollback editing","description":"# Rope for Large Text Manipulation

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement a Rope data structure for efficient manipulation of large text sequences using an arena-based balanced binary tree of string chunks.

## Data Structure
- Balanced binary tree where leaves hold string chunks
- O(log n) insert, delete, split, concat operations
- O(log n) index-to-character lookup
- Automatic rebalancing to maintain O(log n) depth

## Use Cases in FrankenTerm
- Scrollback buffer management for large terminal outputs
- Efficient text editing for terminal replay
- Fast substring extraction for search results
- Memory-efficient text storage with structural sharing

 {"id":"ft-283h4.31","title":"Implement disjoint_intervals.rs — sorted non-overlapping interval set with merge-on-insert","description":"# Disjoint Intervals

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement a sorted set of non-overlapping intervals that automatically merges overlapping insertions.

## Use Cases
- Track active time windows for pane sessions
- Gap detection in telemetry streams
- Coalesce overlapping output capture ranges
- Resource occupancy tracking

## API Surface
- DisjointIntervals::new()
- insert(start, end) — add interval, merge overlaps
- contains(point) — point-in-range query
- intersects(start, end) — range overlap test
- gaps() — iterator over uncovered ranges
- span() — total covered length
- intervals() — iterate all intervals
- remove(start, end) — punch hole
- stats() / serde roundtrip

## Testing
- 25+ inline unit tests
- 30+ proptest properties","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:32:42.663162Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T02:35:50.516795Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T02:35:50.516744Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.31","depends_on_id":"ft-283h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:32:42.663162Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"ft-283h4.33","title":"Implement suffix_array.rs — suffix array with LCP for O(m log n) substring search","description":"# Suffix Array with LCP Array

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement suffix array construction and LCP (Longest Common Prefix) array for efficient full-text substring search.

## Use Cases in FrankenTerm
- Fast substring search in terminal scrollback output

 {"id":"ft-283h4.38","title":"Implement lfu_cache.rs — O(1) LFU eviction cache for resource management","description":"# LFU Cache (Least Frequently Used)

## Alien Artifact Track (ft-283h4)

Implement an O(1) LFU cache using the O(1) LFU algorithm from Shah et al. (2010).

## Use Cases
- Evict least-used pane data under memory pressure
- Cache hot patterns/commands based on access frequency
- Resource pool management for infrequently used connections
- Complement to existing LRU cache for frequency-based policies

## API Surface
- LfuCache::new(capacity) — create with max capacity
- get(key) — lookup, increments frequency
- insert(key, value) — insert, evicts LFU if full
- remove(key) — explicit remove
- contains(key) — check without incrementing frequency
- len() / capacity / is_full
- stats() / config / serde roundtrip

## Testing
- 25+ inline unit tests
- 30+ proptest properties","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:50:09.363025Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T02:50:14.604054Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-283h4.38","depends_on_id":"ft-283h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-16T02:50:09.363025Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
